title: triplaris gardneriana
intro: Triplaris gardneriana, also known as "Cinchona-like tree" or "Sapucaia", is a tropical plant species native to South America, particularly in the countries of Brazil, Colombia, and Peru. The medicinal properties of Triplaris gardneriana lie in its bark, which contains alkaloids such as quinovic acid and quinovic acid glycoside, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and treat fever. Herbal preparations of Triplaris gardneriana include infusions and decoctions made from the bark, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
